text: An additional 5mm port is placed in the patient's left upper quadrant to aid in the manipulation of the massively distended gallbladder.
The surgeon initiates a medial-first dissection of the gallbladder using robotic instruments, focusing on the area closest to the liver.
The surgeon uses robotic instruments to dissect the gallbladder from the cystic plate laterally, following a medial dissection approach.
The surgeon continues to mobilize the gallbladder, detaching it from the cystic plate using lateral dissection techniques.
The surgeon fully mobilizes the gallbladder, ensuring it is detached from surrounding structures, while the surgical team prepares the necessary setup in the operating room.
The surgeon mobilizes the artery to enhance the critical view of the surgical field, facilitating better visualization of the anatomical structures.
The surgeon continues to dissect the gallbladder from the liver, progressing towards complete separation of the gallbladder from the liver tissue.
The surgeon continues the dissection of the gallbladder, moving from medial to lateral across the liver bed.
The surgeon completes the dissection of the gallbladder from the liver on the lateral side, finalizing its mobilization.
The gallbladder is fully dissociated from the liver, indicating the completion of its dissection from the liver bed.
The surgeon tidies up the dissection area around the gallbladder, ensuring clean separation from the liver without extending the dissection too low.
The surgeon clips and transects the cystic artery, then prepares to externalize the gallbladder through a small mini laparotomy incision.
The surgeon fillets the gallbladder open, tracing it down to the cystic duct orifice, where the gallbladder is transected.
The surgeon removes the gallbladder specimen through the small mini laparotomy incision.
The surgeon completes the procedure with a standard closure of the incision site in layers using a small bite technique, ensuring no spillage from the surgical site.
